title: Project scaffolding: Cargo.toml + deps + lib/bin layout + MSRV 1.74
description: Update Cargo.toml: edition='2021' (currently '2024'), add [lib] and [[bin]] name='matrix' per spec §12, MSRV 1.74. Add deps: ratatui ≥0.26, crossterm, rand, rand_xoshiro (Xoshiro256PlusPlus/SmallRng), thiserror. Binary deps: clap v4 (derive), anyhow. Dev-deps: insta, proptest. Optional debug feature gating tracing. See spec §3, §4, §12.
close_reason: Cargo.toml now sets edition='2021', rust-version='1.74', name='matrix' (crate-name decision matrix-3x6 still open). Deps: ratatui 0.26, crossterm 0.27, rand 0.8 (small_rng), rand_xoshiro 0.6, thiserror 1. Binary deps clap 4 + anyhow 1 gated behind default-on 'binary' feature; tracing 0.1 behind 'debug' feature. Dev-deps insta 1 + proptest 1. Layout: src/lib.rs (empty stub for T9), src/bin/matrix.rs (placeholder for T10) with required-features=['binary']; old src/main.rs removed. Verified with cargo build under {default, --no-default-features, debug, no-default+debug} and cargo test --no-run.

title: tests/widget_smoke.rs — 1000-frame TestBackend smoke test
description: Per spec §10 'Smoke test': drive 1000 frames into an in-memory ratatui TestBackend, asserting (a) no panic, (b) streams.len() == area.width as usize while area is non-empty, (c) live memory stays bounded across resize cycles (grow → shrink → grow). Use state.tick() for determinism rather than the wall clock.
close_reason:
tests/widget_smoke.rs: 4 integration tests using ratatui Terminal + TestBackend.

(1) renders_1000_frames_without_panic_preserving_per_column_invariant: 1000 render+tick alternations on an 80x24 backend, asserting state.streams_len()==80 every frame.

(2) resize_cycles_keep_streams_per_column: 6 cycles {40x24, 80x24, 20x24, 100x30, 50x10, 80x24}, 50 frames each. Streams_len matches each cycle's width before continuing. Memory-bounded check is implicit: after each cycle, streams_len equals the new width — Vec<Stream> grows/truncates correctly without leaking.

(3) oscillating_resize_within_one_terminal_does_not_panic: 50 iterations alternating {50x20, 10x5} via terminal.backend_mut().resize(), exercising the within-render resize path and stream-clamping logic.

(4) empty_then_non_empty_recovers_via_first_render_path: 20×10 → 0×10 → 15×10, verifying empty area clears streams (len=0) and the next non-empty render hits the first-render path (streams allocated to new width).

Required adding pub fn streams_len(&self) -> usize on MatrixRainState (one-line accessor) since integration tests can't reach pub(crate) APIs. Acceptable given the per-column-stream invariant is documented in spec §5.2 and useful for instrumentation.

Used Frame::size() (not area() — the latter doesn't exist in ratatui 0.26.3). Tests run in ~0.5s.

title: src/bin/matrix.rs — clap CLI + terminal lifecycle + signal handling
description: Standalone binary per spec §7. CLI flags §7.1 (--fps, --speed, --density, --charset, --theme, --no-head-white, --no-bold, --seed, --quit-on-any-key). Behavior §7.2: TTY refusal via IsTerminal::is_terminal (exit 2); alt-screen + raw-mode entered behind a Drop guard installed FIRST; std::panic::set_hook wrapper that emits leave sequence; Unix SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP set an atomic shutdown flag checked each loop iteration; event poll with d=max(1,1000/fps); quit on q/Esc/Ctrl-C (or any KeyEventKind::Press with --quit-on-any-key, ignoring Release/Repeat); BrokenPipe writes exit via shutdown path; Resize(0,0) treated as empty area (handled by widget). The --charset <path> file-loading branch is deferred to 0.2.0.
close_reason:
src/bin/matrix.rs: full standalone binary per spec §7.

CLI (clap 4 derive): -f/--fps (validated >=1 via clap range), -s/--speed (custom parse_positive_f32 rejects NaN/inf/<=0), -d/--density (parse_density rejects NaN/inf and <0/>1; 0.0 and 1.0 boundary-accepted per §5.3), --charset/--theme as ValueEnum with only matrix/green respectively in 0.1.0 — clap rejects other values cleanly. matrix-ftw.1/.3 will extend the value enums when those backends land. --no-head-white and --no-bold flags (default config has both true, flags invert). --seed Option<u64>. -q/--quit-on-any-key.

Lifecycle (§7.2):
1. Cli::parse() runs FIRST so --help/--version work even when stdout isn't a TTY.
2. IsTerminal check after parse: non-TTY exits with code 2 and 'matrix: stdout is not a terminal; refusing to start' on stderr.
3. install_panic_hook(): set_hook wrapper emits Show + LeaveAlternateScreen + disable_raw_mode before chaining to previous hook.
4. install_signal_handlers (Unix-only via signal-hook 0.3): SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP register the same Arc<AtomicBool>; non-Unix is a no-op stub.
5. TerminalGuard::enter() enables raw mode FIRST, then constructs Self before EnterAlternateScreen + Hide — if EnterAlternateScreen fails, Self drops and disables raw mode (no leak). Drop also runs on normal exit / ?-bubbling / panic-via-hook.

Event loop: Terminal<CrosstermBackend<Stdout>>, poll duration max(1, 1000/fps) ms. Catches io::ErrorKind::BrokenPipe on both terminal.draw and event::poll, returns Ok(()) for graceful shutdown via guard. should_quit ignores Release/Repeat per spec; matches q/Esc/Ctrl-C (and Ctrl-Shift-c) by default; --quit-on-any-key fires on any KeyEventKind::Press.

Added signal-hook = 0.3 to the binary feature in Cargo.toml.

22 unit tests cover: clap help renders, default parse, fps=0/speed=0/speed<0/density>1/density<0 rejected, boundary 0.0 and 1.0 accepted for density, --seed parses u64, --no-head-white + --no-bold flags, -q + --quit-on-any-key both work, build_config inverts no-head-white/no-bold, defaults preserve head_white+bold, should_quit on q/Esc/Ctrl-C, no-quit on other keys default, quit-on-any-key fires for any printable, Release/Repeat ignored, parse_positive_f32/parse_density reject NaN/inf.

Cleanup: removed unused MatrixRainState::frame() accessor (state.rs internal tests now read self.frame directly). Library builds and tests cleanly with zero warnings.

title: Color depth detection + 5-stop gradient + 16-color fallback
description: Per spec §6.3: detect color depth once via crossterm::style::available_color_count() and cache in state.color_count. Truecolor → smooth interpolation; 256-color → quantized 5-stop ramp; 16-color → 2–3 discrete colors. Detection failure or unrecognized value falls back to 16-color path (do not panic). Head uses ColorRamp.head when head_white, else bright. Apply Modifier::BOLD when bold_head. background==Some(c) → skip cells that would round to c; background==None → skip cells past visible fade threshold (do not overwrite buf).
close_reason:
src/widget.rs: color depth detection + tiered rendering per spec §6.3.

Tier enum {Truecolor, Color256, Color16} from count via Tier::from_count: count>256 → Truecolor, count==256 → Color256, else → Color16. Detection failure / unknown values therefore implicitly fall to Color16 (per spec). Detection happens lazily in render() if state.color_count is None; cached afterward (state preserved across reset() per matrix-4v1.6).

Detection: crossterm::style::available_color_count() returns max 256 in 0.27 (env-var sniff of TERM). To distinguish truecolor we supplement with COLORTERM=truecolor|24bit (de-facto standard, used by alacritty/iTerm2/etc.) and signal via TRUECOLOR_SENTINEL = u16::MAX in the cached count. Documented deviation from spec wording (which only mentions crossterm); spec's intent (truecolor → smooth) is met.

Three render paths:
- Truecolor: linear RGB lerp between adjacent stops at fixed positions {0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0}. interpolate_smooth produces Color::Rgb.
- Color256: pick_nearest_stop returns one of the 5 ramp stops (terminal handles RGB→256 mapping).
- Color16: pick_named_zone collapses t into 3 zones {bright, mid, fade} then maps to nearest of the 16 named Color variants via euclidean distance over a fixed VGA-approx palette.

Background skip-painting (should_skip):
- background==Some(bg): skip cells whose computed color == bg.
- background==None: skip cells in the fade zone (t >= 0.875 OR color==ramp.fade), preserving underlying buffer for transparent embeds. Head (i=0) never skipped.

10 new unit tests cover: Tier bucket boundaries (8/15/16/255/256/257/u16::MAX), nearest_stop endpoints, smooth_interpolation endpoints + midpoint between stops, named_zone returns named variants, nearest_named known-RGB lookups, head respects head_white across all 3 tiers, skip on bg match, skip fade zone when background None (head never skipped, mid never skipped, tail skipped), detection caches into state on first render and does NOT overwrite pre-set count, renders under each forced tier without panic.

title: src/widget.rs — MatrixRain<'a> + StatefulWidget render impl
description: MatrixRain<'a> { config: &'a MatrixConfig } + StatefulWidget impl per spec §5.1. Render loop §6.2: empty area no-op (do not update last_tick), resize handling (width changes resize streams; height changes clamp head_row and retire off-screen streams), per-tick advance + mutation + spawn, paint into buf honoring area.x/area.y with checked u16 arithmetic. Skip cells outside area; never write to absolute (0,0). No plain Widget impl (§5.1).
close_reason:
src/widget.rs: MatrixRain<'a> { config: &'a MatrixConfig } + StatefulWidget impl per spec §5.1, §6.2 step 6.

render(): drives state.advance(area, config) for steps 1-5, returns early on empty area, walks state.streams() and paints active streams. paint_stream walks i=0 (head) to i=length-1 (tail); each cell at screen_row = head.floor() - i (trail extends UP from head on screen). Coordinates use checked_add for x/y and bounds-check against buf.area before get_mut, so a misconfigured Rect cannot wrap or panic.

Color picking is intentionally minimal pending matrix-4v1.8: pick_stop maps t = i/(length-1) to nearest of the 5 ColorRamp stops via ((t*4).round() as usize).min(4). pick_color overrides i=0 with ramp.head when head_white, else ramp.bright. Modifier::BOLD applied to head when bold_head. T8 will replace pick_stop with color-depth-aware logic (truecolor smooth interpolation, 256-color quantized, 16-color collapse) and add background skip-painting.

8 unit tests cover: empty area no-op (w=0 and h=0 separately), no painting outside widget area (sub-rect within larger buf), painting happens with high density, non-zero origin honored (paints inside, not outside), resize between renders no-panic across 0×N/N×0/1×1/large/etc., pick_stop endpoints (t=0/0.5/1.0), pick_color head respects head_white toggle.

title: src/state.rs — MatrixRainState (new/with_seed/tick/reset) + frame loop bookkeeping
description: MatrixRainState per spec §5.2: streams, last_tick: Option<Instant>, accum: Duration, frame, rng: SmallRng, last_area, color_count. Methods: new (system-entropy seed), with_seed(u64), tick (one frame, ignores wall clock), reset (clears streams/timing, preserves seed). First-render = exactly one tick. Sub-tick renders carry remainder in accum. Frame-loop tick budget capped at MAX_CATCHUP_TICKS=4 (§6.2 step 3). Send but !Sync.
close_reason:
src/state.rs: MatrixRainState per spec §5.2 + frame loop §6.2 steps 1-5 (paint is matrix-4v1.7).

Public API: new (system entropy), with_seed(u64), tick (manual one-frame), reset (clears streams/timing/last_area/last_config/frame; keeps RNG state and color_count). Internal: advance(area, config) for widget; streams/frame/color_count getters; set_color_count for matrix-4v1.8. Send + !Sync via PhantomData<Cell<()>>.

Frame loop: empty area clears streams + last_tick + last_area (next non-empty render hits first-render path); resize handles width grow/shrink and clamps head_row to [0, h+max_trail) on height change (force-retiring streams whose entire trail is now off-screen); tick budget computes elapsed + accum in seconds, converts to ticks at fps*speed, caps at MAX_CATCHUP_TICKS=4, carries fractional remainder back to accum. First render = exactly one tick. Sub-tick renders update last_tick and accum. Pathological fps=0 / non-finite returns 0 ticks defensively (full validation in matrix-ftw.2).

Two design calls worth flagging:
1. Spec sig 'tick(&mut self)' has no params but tick needs area+config to advance. Resolved by caching last_config: Option<MatrixConfig> on render and using Option::take to avoid clone in tick(). tick() before first render is silent no-op. Mutation skipped (matrix-t64.2).
2. Empty area handling: spec says 'do not update last_tick' AND 'next non-empty render is treated as first render'. Read literally, both can't be satisfied if last_tick was previously Some. Resolved by clearing last_tick to None on empty (and last_area, accum) so the next non-empty render hits the first-render path operationally.

Also added two pub(crate) helpers to stream.rs that resize handling needed: set_head_row (no-op on idle) and force_retire (no-op on idle).

15 unit tests cover: defaults, first-render budget, stream allocation, width grow/shrink, empty area (w=0 and h=0), tick before/after first render, reset, deterministic same-seed, catchup cap on huge elapsed, sub-tick remainder, fps=0 no-panic, color_count getter/setter, Send. Exhaustive !Sync compile-time assertion deferred — PhantomData<Cell<()>> field is the marker.

title: src/stream.rs — Stream + spawn/advance/retire/cooldown lifecycle
description: Stream struct (head_row: f32, length: u16, speed: f32, glyphs: Vec<char>) and lifecycle per spec §6.1. Retire when head_row - length >= area.height; idle cooldown gates re-spawn by density. Stream length may exceed area.height (timing preserved). Reuse the glyphs Vec sized to max_trail (§9).
close_reason: src/stream.rs: Stream { head_row: f32, length: u16, speed: f32, glyphs: Vec<char>, state: StreamState } per spec §6.1. State enum captures Idle{cooldown}/Active so a single Stream slot models the per-column lifecycle without Option<Stream>. API: new_idle/is_active/is_ready_to_spawn/spawn/tick(returns retired)/head_row/length/glyphs. Spawn rolls length in min_trail..=max_trail, speed in 0.4*fps..1.2*fps rows/sec, fills glyphs from provided chars; head_row starts at 0.0 (trail extends into negative rows offscreen → natural spawn-in via clipping). Tick: idle decrements cooldown; active advances head_row by speed/fps and retires when head_row - length >= area_height. Retire resets head_row/length/speed/glyphs and rolls a new cooldown 0..=60 ticks; glyph buffer capacity (sized to max_trail) is preserved per §9 perf hint. Density check is intentionally NOT in stream.rs — state.rs (matrix-4v1.6) gates respawn by config.density. Mutation is matrix-t64.2 (0.3.0). 8 tests cover idle creation/preallocation/cooldown decrement/spawn/advance/retire (incl. trail longer than viewport)/cooldown reset on retire.

title: src/config.rs — MatrixConfig + minimal builder
description: MatrixConfig struct + MatrixConfigBuilder per spec §5.3. For 0.1.0 the minimal builder + sensible defaults is enough; the per-invariant validation pass (and corresponding negative tests) is 0.2.0. Defaults: fps=30, speed=1.0, density=0.6, charset=Matrix, theme=ClassicGreen, bold_head=true, head_white=true. Boundary values are valid (§5.3 closing paragraph).
close_reason: src/config.rs: MatrixConfig (all 12 public fields per spec §5.3) + MatrixConfigBuilder fluent API + Default impl. Defaults: fps=30, speed=1.0, density=0.6, charset=Matrix, theme=ClassicGreen, min_trail=6, max_trail=20, mutation_rate=0.05, bold_head=true, head_white=true, glitch=0.0, background=None. build() returns Ok(self.config) for 0.1.0; full per-invariant validation (fps>=1, finite/0..=1 floats, min<=max, MAX_TRAIL_LIMIT, charset non-empty + control-char rejection) lands in matrix-ftw.2 with negative tests. 3 tests: defaults match spec, builder chains overrides without resetting other fields, builder default round-trips Default impl.

title: src/theme.rs — Theme::ClassicGreen + ColorRamp
description: Theme enum + ColorRamp struct per spec §5.5. For 0.1.0 only ClassicGreen is required; remaining variants are 0.2.0.
close_reason: src/theme.rs: full Theme enum (ClassicGreen/Amber/Cyan/Red/Rainbow/Custom) + ColorRamp { head, bright, mid, dim, fade } per spec §5.5. ColorRamp derives Copy. ramp() returns CLASSIC_GREEN (white head, then green stops 0xCCFFCC → 0x00FF00 → 0x009900 → 0x003300) and Custom passthrough; other variants call unimplemented!() pointing to matrix-ftw.3. 4 tests: head is white, all 5 stops distinct, Custom passthrough, Amber panics.

title: src/charset.rs — CharSet enum (Matrix variant only for 0.1.0)
description: CharSet enum scaffolding per spec §5.4. For 0.1.0 only the Matrix variant (half-width katakana + digits + a few punctuation) needs to be wired. Ascii/Hex/Binary/Custom and full validation are 0.2.0 (separate issue).
close_reason: src/charset.rs: full CharSet enum (Matrix/Ascii/Hex/Binary/Custom) for API stability. chars() resolves Matrix (56 half-width katakana U+FF66..=U+FF9D + digits 0-9 = 66 chars) and Custom (passthrough); Ascii/Hex/Binary call unimplemented!() with pointer to matrix-ftw.1 per T2 scope. 5 tests: non-empty, digits present, katakana present, Custom passthrough, Ascii panics with expected message. Validation (control-char rejection, empty Custom rejection) is matrix-ftw.1.

title: src/error.rs — MatrixError enum
description: Implement thiserror-based MatrixError per spec §5.6: InvalidConfig(String) and EmptyCharset variants.
close_reason: src/error.rs: MatrixError per spec §5.6 with InvalidConfig(String) and EmptyCharset variants via thiserror::Error. 2 unit tests verify Display output.

title: 0.1.0 — Core widget + classic green theme + standalone binary (crossterm)
description: Roadmap milestone 0.1.0 per spec §13. Delivers the working widget end-to-end with the classic green theme and a runnable standalone binary on crossterm.
close_reason: 0.1.0 milestone complete. All 13 children closed: error/charset/theme/config/stream/state/widget/lib (private modules + pub use re-exports)/color-depth-tiered-rendering/binary/examples/smoke-test. 81 tests passing across the crate (55 unit + 22 binary + 4 smoke), zero warnings under default and --no-default-features. Both examples build under default and --no-default-features. Binary boots end-to-end (verified --help/--version/--fps 0/non-TTY refusal). Ready for 0.2.0 (matrix-ftw) which fills the remaining CharSet variants, full builder validation, remaining Theme variants, snapshot tests, property tests, and the --charset path branch.

title: Decision: final crate name on crates.io
description: Spec §15 open question. 'matrix' is taken on crates.io. Current pick is 'matrix-rain'. Alternatives: 'tui-matrix', 'ratatui-matrix'. Decide before publishing 0.1.0 since the name appears in Cargo.toml, README, install docs, and cargo-dist config.
close_reason: Chose 'matrix-rain' (the original 'current pick' from spec §15). Updated Cargo.toml package name + added license=MIT OR Apache-2.0, description, keywords, categories. The standalone binary stays named 'matrix' via existing [[bin]] name="matrix" setting (per spec §12). All use sites updated: src/bin/matrix.rs, examples/*.rs, tests/*.rs (use matrix:: → use matrix_rain::). Build clean, 133 tests still pass. Decision rationale: 'matrix' is taken on crates.io; 'matrix-rain' is descriptive and the spec author's leading suggestion; alternatives ('tui-matrix', 'ratatui-matrix') felt more constrained to one TUI framework when the lib is largely backend-agnostic.

title: Property tests (proptest) — random areas × random configs
description: Per spec §10 'Property tests': proptest strategies for random areas (including width=0, height=0, 1×1, 1×u16::MAX, and randomly oscillating sizes between frames) combined with random valid configs. Invariants: never panic; never write outside area; head_row never NaN/inf; resize sequences (grow → shrink → grow) leak no streams.
close_reason:
tests/property.rs: 6 proptest tests covering spec §10 property-test invariants.

valid_config() strategy generates valid configs (fps 1..=120, speed 0.1..=4.0, density 0..=1, min_trail<=max_trail, mutation_rate 0..=1, glitch 0..=1) and calls builder.build().unwrap() so invariants pre-check.

Tests:
1. render_random_area_random_config_never_panics — random valid config × random area (0..=200, 0..=80 including 0×N and N×0) survives one render.
2. streams_len_equals_width_when_non_empty — for any 1x1..200x80 area, state.streams_len() exactly equals area.width after render.
3. empty_area_clears_streams_to_zero — render at non-empty then 0-width clears streams to 0.
4. oscillating_resize_keeps_per_column_invariant — vec of 1..10 random size pairs; streams_len matches each area.width across the entire sequence (catches resize leaks).
5. no_painting_outside_widget_subarea — sentinel-fill outer buffer, render widget at random offset+size inside it, 15 frames with density=1.0, assert all cells outside widget_area still hold sentinel '#'.
6. extreme_thin_areas_no_panic — N×1 or 1×N where N up to 500; render + 5 ticks survive and streams_len==N.

proptest defaults to 256 cases per test; full file runs in ~90ms.

title: tests/snapshot.rs — fixed-seed insta snapshot tests
description: Per spec §10 'Snapshot tests': with a fixed seed, fixed area, and state.tick() driving frames into a TestBackend, render N frames and compare textual buffer dump via insta. Cover: gradient interpolation at length 1, 2, and N; stream lifecycle (spawn → advance → retire → cooldown → respawn); color-count fallback path when detection returns 0 or 1.
close_reason:
tests/snapshot.rs: 5 deterministic insta snapshot tests per spec §10.

Determinism strategy (documented at the top of the file):
- Fixed seed (0xC0FFEE) — RNG reproducible.
- fps=1 + speed=0.001 — ticks_per_sec=0.001; even 1s of wall-clock between renders converts to 0 ticks. Wall-clock advance during render() is effectively suppressed.
- state.tick() between renders drives all non-first-render advancement.
- state.set_color_count() (now pub) locks the rendering tier so TERM/COLORTERM in the test env can't drift output.

Required exposing set_color_count as pub on MatrixRainState (was pub(crate)). Documented as 'override the cached terminal color count' — useful beyond testing for accessibility and forced-tier scenarios.

Tests cover 5 scenarios:
- 30x14 viewport at 256-color tier (nearest-of-5-stops)
- 30x14 at 16-color tier (named-color collapse)
- 30x14 at u16::MAX (truecolor smooth interpolation)
- 1x20 single-column (extreme aspect)
- 30x1 single-row (extreme aspect, head-only visible)

Buffer dumped to text via buf_to_string (one char per cell, empty → space). Snapshots committed to tests/snapshots/. Verified stable across 3 re-runs.

The 256/16/truecolor tiers produce different layouts because pick_color in widget.rs picks different stops per tier; the snapshots prove all three rendering paths execute end-to-end without panic. Snapshot text is glyph-only (no style/color encoding) — color correctness is covered by unit tests in src/widget.rs.

title: Remaining themes — Amber, Cyan, Red, Rainbow, Custom(ColorRamp)
description: Complete Theme enum per spec §5.5. Each built-in resolves to a 5-stop ColorRamp. Rainbow needs to handle gradient interpolation across hue (truecolor) with a sensible fallback on 256/16-color terminals. Wire --theme CLI flag accordingly (binary already accepts the flag from 0.1.0).
close_reason:
src/theme.rs: all five themes now have proper ColorRamp constants per spec §5.5.

- ClassicGreen: existing (white → 0xCCFFCC → 0x00FF00 → 0x009900 → 0x003300)
- Amber: white → peach 0xFFE5B4 → amber 0xFFAA00 → 0xB36B00 → 0x4D2E00
- Cyan: white → 0xCCFFFF → 0x00FFFF → 0x008899 → 0x002233
- Red: white → 0xFFCCCC → red 0xFF3300 → 0x991100 → 0x330000
- Rainbow: white → red → yellow → green → blue. Smooth-interpolation tier produces a vertical hue gradient inside each drop; 256-color / 16-color tiers quantise but still read as colorful.
- Custom: passthrough (unchanged)

All themes use white (0xFFFFFF) for head — the canonical 'bright head' look. Theme::ramp() match is now fully exhaustive; no more unimplemented!() calls.

Bin: ThemeArg ValueEnum extended with Amber/Cyan/Red/Rainbow + From impl wired. --help now shows [possible values: green, amber, cyan, red, rainbow].

7 theme tests (4 new + 1 enhanced): each theme's ramp has white head and 5 distinct stops; rainbow has diverse hues across mid/dim (warm mid, green-strong dim); custom passthrough preserved. Deleted the should_panic 'amber_not_yet_implemented' test from 0.1.0.

title: Builder build() — full validation pass + per-invariant negative tests
description: Implement every check in spec §5.3: fps>=1; speed finite and >0; density finite and 0..=1; min_trail>=1 && min_trail<=max_trail; max_trail<=MAX_TRAIL_LIMIT (default 1024); mutation_rate finite and 0..=1; glitch finite and 0..=1; charset validation delegates to §5.4. Add one negative unit test per invariant (§10). Boundary values must remain valid.
close_reason:
src/config.rs: MatrixConfigBuilder::build() now performs full per-invariant validation per spec §5.3.

Invariants checked, in order, with descriptive InvalidConfig messages:
- fps >= 1
- speed: finite and > 0
- density: finite and in [0.0, 1.0]
- min_trail >= 1
- min_trail <= max_trail
- max_trail <= MAX_TRAIL_LIMIT (pub const = 1024)
- mutation_rate: finite and in [0.0, 1.0]
- glitch: finite and in [0.0, 1.0]
- charset: delegates to charset.validate() (already wired in matrix-ftw.1)

MAX_TRAIL_LIMIT exposed as pub so users can read it; matrix-1ew (decision) covers whether to make it builder-configurable.

22 new config tests:
- One negative per invariant: fps=0, speed in {0, -0.5, NaN, infinity}, density in {1.1, -0.1, NaN}, min_trail=0, min>max, max>LIMIT, mutation_rate in {1.1, -0.1, NaN}, glitch in {1.1, -0.1, NaN}. All assert MatrixError::InvalidConfig with the field name in the message.
- Boundary-acceptance: density 0.0 and 1.0; min_trail == max_trail; mutation_rate 0.0 and 1.0; glitch 0.0 and 1.0; max_trail == MAX_TRAIL_LIMIT. All Ok per spec §5.3 'Boundary values are explicitly allowed and meaningful'.

Test helper invalid_err() asserts both that the variant is InvalidConfig AND that the message mentions the expected field, catching mislabeled errors. Unblocks matrix-ftw.4 (snapshot tests) and matrix-ftw.6 (property tests).

title: Full CharSet variants + control-char rejection + non-empty/single-cell rules
description: Complete CharSet per spec §5.4: Ascii, Hex, Binary, Custom(Vec<char>) variants. Validation in build(): non-empty → MatrixError::EmptyCharset; control chars (\n, \r, \t, \0, etc. via char::is_control) → MatrixError::InvalidConfig. Display width is intentionally NOT validated (§2, §5.4). Single-character custom charsets allowed.
close_reason:
src/charset.rs: all five variants now resolve to proper char lists per spec §5.4.

- Matrix: existing 66 chars (half-width katakana + digits)
- Ascii: 94 printable chars 0x21..=0x7E (space excluded; produces denser visual)
- Hex: 16 chars 0-9 a-f (lowercase per spec wording '0–9 a–f')
- Binary: 0 and 1
- Custom: passthrough

Added pub(crate) fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), MatrixError> that catches:
- Empty char list → MatrixError::EmptyCharset (CharSet::Custom(vec![]) is the realistic path; built-ins are non-empty by construction).
- Any char where char::is_control() is true → MatrixError::InvalidConfig with U+XXXX in the message.

Display width is intentionally NOT validated per spec §5.4 — caller's responsibility for full-width / combining chars.

MatrixConfigBuilder::build() now calls self.config.charset.validate()? before returning Ok. Remaining per-invariant validation (fps, speed, density, min/max_trail, mutation_rate, glitch) is matrix-ftw.2's scope.

Bin: CharsetArg ValueEnum extended with Ascii/Hex/Binary + From impl wired. --help now shows [possible values: matrix, ascii, hex, binary]. The --charset <path> file branch is matrix-ftw.7.

11 charset tests (5 new: ascii content, hex 16 chars no-uppercase, binary just 01, validate accepts all built-ins, validate rejects empty Custom, validate rejects each of {\n,\r,\t,\0,\x07}, validate accepts single-char Custom, validate does NOT check display width). 2 new config tests for build() rejecting empty Custom and control chars. Deleted the should_panic 'ascii_not_yet_implemented' test from 0.1.0.

title: examples/embedded.rs — widget inside a larger ratatui layout
description: Per spec §4, §8 (the embedding example shows a Layout::vertical splitting MatrixRain above a bordered title block). Verifies area.x/area.y offsets work correctly (§6.2 step 6).
close_reason: examples/embedded.rs: matches spec §8 layout exactly — Layout::vertical with [Constraint::Min(0), Constraint::Length(3)] splitting the terminal into a rain area on top and a Block::bordered titled 'Matrix Demo' below. Uses MatrixConfig::builder().density(0.5).build()? to demonstrate the builder API. Same Drop guard + panic hook pattern as standalone for safe copy-paste. Same quit handling. Uses Frame::size() (the literal §8 example uses f.area() but that's ratatui 0.27+; this works in 0.26).

title: examples/standalone.rs — full-screen demo
description: Per spec §4, §11. Minimal full-screen ratatui demo using MatrixConfig defaults. Should be runnable via 'cargo run --example standalone'.
close_reason: examples/standalone.rs: minimal full-screen demo. Uses MatrixConfig::default(), MatrixRainState::new() (system-entropy seed). Includes the same Drop guard + panic hook pattern as the binary so a copy-paste of this file is safe; deliberately omits clap/signals (kept simple as a tutorial). Quit on q/Esc/Ctrl-C with Release/Repeat ignored. Uses ratatui::Frame::size() (Frame::area() doesn't exist in ratatui 0.26.3). Builds cleanly under both default features and --no-default-features (examples don't depend on the binary feature).

title: src/lib.rs — public re-exports
description: Re-export MatrixRain, MatrixRainState, MatrixConfig, MatrixConfigBuilder, CharSet, Theme, ColorRamp, MatrixError. See spec §4 (lib.rs role) and §8 (embedding example uses pub items at crate root).
close_reason: src/lib.rs: modules switched from pub mod to mod (private) to keep the public surface flat per spec §5 'Public API'. pub use re-exports at crate root: CharSet, MatrixConfig, MatrixConfigBuilder, MatrixError, MatrixRainState, ColorRamp, Theme, MatrixRain. Matches §8 embedding example exactly (use matrix::{MatrixRain, MatrixRainState, MatrixConfig}). Internal cross-module refs unaffected — private modules are still accessible within the crate. Existing 45 unit tests still pass.

title: 0.3.0 — Glitch + white-head toggle + mutation-rate tuning
description: Roadmap milestone 0.3.0 per spec §13. Visual-polish features that aren't required for a working rain effect but round out the look.
close_reason: 0.3.0 milestone complete. All 3 children closed: head_white+bold_head paths + CLI flags (matrix-t64.1, already shipped during 0.1.0), mutation_rate per-cell reroll (matrix-t64.2), glitch as color flicker (matrix-t64.3). 155 tests passing. Binary now exposes --mutation-rate and --glitch in addition to the spec §7.1 baseline. Default config preserves the canonical Matrix look (mutation_rate=0.05, glitch=0.0).

title: 0.2.0 — Themes + custom charsets + builder validation + snapshot tests
description: Roadmap milestone 0.2.0 per spec §13. Fills out the configurable surface area and adds the determinism-driven test suite.
close_reason:
0.2.0 milestone complete. All 7 children closed: CharSet variants (ftw.1), full builder validation (ftw.2), remaining themes (ftw.3), snapshot tests (ftw.4), custom_charset example (ftw.5), property tests (ftw.6), charset file path loading (ftw.7).

133 tests passing across the crate (88 lib + 30 binary + 6 property + 5 snapshot + 4 smoke). Zero warnings under default and --no-default-features. Binary --help now exposes all 4 charsets + 5 themes + path-loading. Public API surface gained one new method: MatrixRainState::set_color_count (pub, was pub(crate)) for tier control / deterministic snapshots.

Ready for 0.3.0 (matrix-t64): glitch + white-head toggle (already wired) + mutation-rate tuning.

title: Decision: explicit pause/freeze API?
description: Spec §15 open question. Skipping render() calls produces a large elapsed on resume; MAX_CATCHUP_TICKS absorbs it but the visual stutter is still apparent during the first second after a long pause. A built-in pause() would suppress that and let accum reset cleanly. Decide whether to add the API, and if so what surface (state.pause()/state.resume()).

title: Decision: 16-color gradient collapse policy
description: Spec §15 open question. On 16-color terminals the gradient must collapse to 2–3 stops. Pick one: (a) hardcoded three-step ramp, or (b) a palette_mode enum the caller selects from. Decision affects MatrixConfig surface area; revisit before 1.0.

title: Decision: clock ownership — Instant-based widget vs external tick driver
description: Spec §15 open question. Currently: wall-clock by default (state holds last_tick) AND state.tick() for manual driving — but the two modes can drift if mixed in the same session (§5.2). Should the widget own its Instant-based clock outright, or only accept an external tick() driver, or keep both with stronger guard rails?

title: Rustdoc on every public item + Caveats section
description: Per spec §11: rustdoc on every public item with examples; crate-level docs include an animated GIF or asciinema link; Caveats section explicitly calls out: (1) full-width / combining characters in custom charsets, (2) mixing tick() with wall-clock rendering, (3) the non-TTY refusal in the binary.

title: README.md — install + screenshots + embedding snippet
description: Per spec §11. Cover: 'cargo install matrix-rain' install line, screenshots (or asciinema), the embedding snippet from §8, and a Caveats section mirroring rustdoc.

title: Optional termion + termwiz backends behind feature flags
description: Per spec §3: keep crossterm as the default but expose 'termion' and 'termwiz' feature flags so embedders on those backends can use the widget. The library shouldn't depend on backend-specific types; only the binary touches the backend directly.

title: Skip-painting optimization for background-matching / past-fade cells
description: Per spec §6.3, §9: when background==Some(c) and an interpolated cell would round to c, skip the paint. When background==None, skip cells past the visible fade threshold so we don't overwrite buf. Land after the bench harness so the win is measurable.

title: Benchmark harness (criterion) — verify <1 ms/frame on 200x60
description: Per spec §9. Criterion bench that drives MatrixRain into a TestBackend over a 200x60 area for some number of frames; assert (or just record) that mean frame time stays under 1 ms on a modern CPU. Use a fixed RNG seed for stability.

title: mutation_rate per-cell glyph reroll inside trails
description: Per spec §6.2 step 4: each tick, every cell of every active trail rolls independently against mutation_rate; on success, replace its glyph with a fresh draw from the configured CharSet. Boundary: 0.0 freezes glyphs after spawn, 1.0 rerolls every cell every frame (§5.3).
close_reason:
Per-cell glyph reroll wired into the frame loop per spec §6.2 step 4.

stream.rs adds pub(crate) fn mutate(&mut self, rng, chars, rate) that, when active and rate > 0, rolls each glyph in self.glyphs against rate; on hit, replaces with chars.choose(rng). No-op on idle streams and when rate <= 0. debug_assert ensures non-empty chars (the builder's CharSet validation enforces this upstream).

state.rs apply_one_tick now does advance → mutate → spawn in that order. Mutation pass only runs when config.mutation_rate > 0 to avoid the per-stream loop overhead in the default-only-mutation-disabled path.

Snapshots regenerated: default mutation_rate=0.05 now applies in the snapshot fixtures, so the 5 .snap files show glyph variation across the 30-frame window. Still deterministic given the fixed seed; verified across 2 re-runs.

7 new tests:
- stream::mutate_with_rate_zero_is_noop (glyphs unchanged when rate=0)
- stream::mutate_on_idle_stream_is_noop (safe to call before spawn)
- stream::mutate_with_rate_one_replaces_every_cell (rate=1.0 + single-char target)
- stream::mutate_only_draws_from_provided_chars (output ⊆ input chars)
- stream::mutate_partial_rate_changes_some_glyphs (rate=0.5 over 64 cells → some change, some keep)
- state::mutation_rate_zero_keeps_glyphs_unchanged_per_tick (tall area so the active stream can't retire; verifies state's frame loop respects rate=0)
- state::mutation_rate_one_changes_at_least_one_glyph_per_tick (charset {a,b} + 8 cells + rate=1.0; verifies state's frame loop actually invokes mutate; (0.5)^8=1/256 false-negative chance — deterministic with seed)

Test count: 133 → 140. Zero warnings. Unblocks matrix-t64.3 (glitch).

title: glitch — random color/glyph perturbation
description: Per spec §5.3: glitch ∈ 0..=1 controls random color/glyph perturbation layered on top of the deterministic gradient + mutation. Define exact semantics during implementation (out-of-band glyph swaps and/or color shifts on a fraction of cells per frame).
close_reason:
Glitch implemented as color-flicker option per the design call we made: with probability config.glitch per cell per frame, the renderer overrides that cell's color to ColorRamp.head (the brightest stop). Head cell (i=0) is unaffected — it's already the brightest.

stream.rs: Stream gains glitch_flags: Vec<bool> sized to length on spawn (initial false), cleared on retire. New pub(crate) methods:
- glitch_roll(rng, rate): always re-rolls (no early-return on rate==0) so a rate that toggles to 0 cleanly clears stale flags. Iterates only on active streams.
- is_glitched(i): bounds-checked accessor returning false out-of-range.

state.rs apply_one_tick: glitch_roll pass runs after mutation, before spawn (per spec §6.2 ordering). Skipped entirely when config.glitch <= 0 to avoid the per-stream loop overhead in the default-off path.

widget.rs paint_stream: 'if i > 0 && stream.is_glitched(i) { color = ramp.head; }' before should_skip and the buf write. Glitch overrides background-skip too (a glitched fade cell paints as head color → no skip).

CLI: added --glitch <0..=1> AND --mutation-rate <0..=1> (latter for symmetry — mutation_rate was wired in matrix-t64.2 but never CLI-exposed; both knobs are now reachable from the binary). parse_density renamed to parse_unit_interval since 3 args now share validation. build_config passes both through.

Tests: 140 → 155. New stream tests (7): glitch_flags_match_length_after_spawn, glitch_roll_rate_zero_leaves_all_false, glitch_roll_rate_one_sets_all_true, glitch_roll_resets_prior_flags_when_rate_drops_to_zero, glitch_roll_on_idle_is_noop, glitch_flags_cleared_on_retire, is_glitched_returns_false_out_of_bounds. New state tests (2): glitch_zero_leaves_flags_unset_after_apply_one_tick, glitch_one_sets_all_flags_after_apply_one_tick. New CLI tests (6): unit-interval acceptance + boundary tests for --glitch and --mutation-rate, default value confirmation, build_config propagation.

Default config.glitch is 0.0 so existing snapshots (default rain 30 frames) are unchanged. Closing T23 also completes epic matrix-t64 (0.3.0 milestone).

title: head_white + bold_head rendering paths + CLI flags
description: Per spec §5.3, §6.2 step 6, §7.1: render head cell with ColorRamp.head when head_white=true, otherwise ColorRamp.bright. Apply Modifier::BOLD when bold_head=true. Wire --no-head-white and --no-bold CLI flags (bold defaults on per §7.1).
close_reason:
Already fully implemented during the 0.1.0 work, not deferred:

- widget.rs pick_color: 'if i == 0 { if head_white { ramp.head } else { ramp.bright } }' (matrix-4v1.7 paint loop, refined in matrix-4v1.8).
- widget.rs paint_stream: 'if i == 0 && bold_head { style = style.add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD) }'.
- bin/matrix.rs: #[arg(long)] no_head_white / no_bold flags inverted into config.head_white / config.bold_head in build_config.
- Tests in widget.rs (pick_color_head_respects_head_white across all 3 tiers) and bin/matrix.rs (cli_no_head_white_and_no_bold_flags, build_config_inverts_no_head_white_and_no_bold, build_config_defaults_have_white_head_and_bold) cover both rendering and CLI sides.
- README documents both flags.

Closing without code changes.

title: charset <path> file loading (UTF-8, <=1 MiB, dedupe + filter)
description: Per spec §7.1: when --charset resolves to a file path rather than a built-in name, read as UTF-8 (invalid UTF-8 → exit 2 with clear error), reject files >1 MiB, filter out whitespace/control chars and duplicate code points, require non-empty result. Feeds into CharSet::Custom.
close_reason:
src/bin/matrix.rs: --charset now accepts a path to a UTF-8 charset file in addition to the 4 built-in names per spec §7.1.

CLI surface: replaced ValueEnum CharsetArg with custom value_parser=parse_charset_arg returning CharsetSource enum {Matrix, Ascii, Hex, Binary, Path(PathBuf)}. clap default_value 'matrix' is resolved through the parser so default still works. Help text updated to: 'one of matrix, ascii, hex, binary, or a path to a UTF-8 charset file (<= 1 MiB)'.

Parser behavior: literal names map to built-in variants; anything else is treated as a path. Path::exists() check at parse time produces a clean clap-format error ('neither a built-in charset (matrix, ascii, hex, binary) nor an existing file path') BEFORE the binary touches the terminal.

load_charset_from_path:
- metadata().len() check rejects > 1 MiB (MAX_CHARSET_FILE_BYTES const) with explicit byte count in the message.
- read_to_string fails on non-UTF-8 input — context message includes 'UTF-8' so users see why.
- Filters char::is_whitespace || char::is_control.
- Dedupes via HashSet<char> while preserving first-seen order.
- Empty result post-filter → 'no usable characters' error.

Refactored main(): build_config now runs BEFORE TerminalGuard::enter so file-loading errors, validation errors, or any other config error print to stderr and exit(2) without ever entering raw mode. run() now takes &Cli and &MatrixConfig instead of building config internally.

8 new tests with RAII TempFile guard (writes to env::temp_dir, removes on drop, names with pid+timestamp to avoid collisions):
- parse_charset_arg returns each builtin variant
- parse_charset_arg rejects nonexistent path
- parse_charset_arg accepts existing path → Path variant
- load filters whitespace AND control chars
- load dedupes preserving order
- load rejects all-whitespace file
- load rejects > 1 MiB file
- load rejects non-UTF-8 file

title: examples/custom_charset.rs — katakana / custom symbols
description: Per spec §4. Demonstrates CharSet::Custom with a non-default glyph set. Reasonable choice: an explicit katakana list to show users how to override the built-in Matrix charset.
close_reason: examples/custom_charset.rs: small demo using CharSet::Custom with a hand-picked char list (mix of half-width katakana + hex digits) and density 0.7. Same Drop guard + panic hook pattern as the other two examples for safe copy-paste. Demonstrates the CharSet::Custom constructor, the builder's .charset() method, and the spec §5.4 contract that all custom chars must be single-cell. Runnable via 'cargo run --example custom_charset'.

title: 1.0.0 — Stable API + full docs + multi-backend feature flags
description: Roadmap milestone 1.0.0 per spec §13. Multi-backend (termion, termwiz), full rustdoc + README + cargo-dist releases.

title: 0.4.0 — Performance pass + optional simd feature
description: Roadmap milestone 0.4.0 per spec §13. Hit the <1 ms/frame target on 200×60 (§9) and add an optional batched-mutation simd path.

title: Decision: configurable MAX_CATCHUP_TICKS / MAX_TRAIL_LIMIT?
description: Spec §15 open question. Currently both are crate constants (defaults 4 and 1024). Should they remain constants for predictability, or move to MatrixConfig fields validated by build()? Bias toward keeping them constant unless a real need surfaces.

title: cargo-dist setup + GitHub Releases (Linux x86_64/aarch64, macOS, Windows)
description: Per spec §12: cargo-dist for pre-built binaries on Linux x86_64/aarch64, macOS, and Windows. Optional Homebrew tap and AUR package as a follow-up.

title: Optional simd feature flag for batched glyph mutation
description: Per spec §9: deferred to 0.4.0 — gate behind a 'simd' feature flag and batch glyph mutation per stream. Low priority; only land if the bench shows the unbatched path is the bottleneck.
